input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the redundancy switchover command do?
target_value: The redundancy switchover command causes the switch to immediately switch over to the Standby Management Module. This command must be executed from the Active Management Module and will fail if the Standby Management Module is in a failed state or not present.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the redundancy switchover command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is BFD?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is BFD Features?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, how do you configure Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route?

slots.feature: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route

target_value: To configure BFD for an IPv4 Static Route, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on an IPv4 static route with the command ip route bfd.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd command do?
target_value: The bfd command enables BFD support on the switch. BFD is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables BFD and removes all related configuration settings. To disable BFD, but retain configuration settings, use the command bfd disable.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command do?
target_value: The bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command enables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. BFD is asynchronous and echo mode is supported. The no form of this command disables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?
target_value: The bfd all-interfaces command enables BFD on all OSPFv2 or OSPFv3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables BFD on all OSPFv2/OSPFv3 or PIM IPv4/IPv6 interfaces, excluding those on which BFD was enabled at the interface level with the commands ip ospf bfd and IPv6 ospfv3 bfd.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd detect-multiplier command do?
target_value: The bfd detect-multiplier command sets BFD detection multiplier on an interface. The no form of this command sets the BFD detection multiplier to the default value of 5.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd disable command do?
target_value: The bfd disable command disables BFD on the switch, but retains all configuration settings.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd disable command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd enable command do?
target_value: The bfd enable command enables or disables BFD for HSC feature.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd echo disable command do?
target_value: The bfd echo disable command disables support for BFD echo packets. Echo packet support is enabled by default. The no form of this command enables support for BFD echo packets. Toggling this feature on 8325 or 8360 switches may cause route flapping.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd echo disable command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd echo-src-ip-address command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd min-receive-interval command do?
target_value: The bfd min-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 milliseconds.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd min-transmit-interval command do?
target_value: The bfd min-transmit-interval command sets the minimum time interval between transmitted BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between transmitted BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 ms.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear bfd statistics command do?
target_value: The clear bfd statistics command clears statistics for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip ospf bfd disable command do?
target_value: The ip ospf bfd disable command disables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd disable command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip route bfd command do?
target_value: The ip route bfd command enables or disables BFD on the specified static route. To disable BFD, issue the command without the bfd option.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip route bfd command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command do?
target_value: The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv3 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv3 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command do?
target_value: The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command disables BFD on the current OSPFv3 interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command?

For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is ERPS?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command do?
target_value: The clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command removes the protection switching and triggers reversion both in revertive and non-revertive operation. This command will not change the configured revertive operation mode.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear erps statistics command do?
target_value: The clear erps statistics command this command clears the ERPS statistics for a ring or a ring instance.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear erps statistics command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring command do?
target_value: The erps ring command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command do?
target_value: The erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command this command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command do?
target_value: The erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command specifies hold-off interval in units of 100 ms. If specified, a defect is not reported immediately. Instead, the hold-off timer is started. On expiration of the timer, if the defect still exists, it is reported to protection switching. The default value for hold-off timer is 0. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the hold-off interval and sets it to the default value of 0.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance command do?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command do?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command do?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command?
For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command do?
target_value: The erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command this command is to configure a sub-ring interconnection node to pass a topology change notification to the ring instance for the parent ring whenever the topology of the sub-ring changes. The parent ring instance performs a Forwarding Database (FDB) flush and sends a protocol message to ensure that other nodes on the parent ring also perform an FDB flush. The no form of this command disables topology change notifications.

target_value: The show erps statistics command this command displays ERPS statistics. The statistics can be displayed for the ring, the instance, or the instance ports.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the interface lag command do?
target_value: The interface lag command createsa Link Aggregation Group(LAG)interfacerepresentedbyanID. The no formofthiscommanddeletesa LAG interfacerepresentedbyanID.

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip address command do?
target_value: The ip address command sets an IPv4 address and subnet mask to a LAG interface. One primary and up to 31 secondary address can be configured per interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface.

For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what SNMP MIB information is documented for Guidelines and limitations?
target_value: For Guidelines and limitations, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations: - For faster convergence of routed traffic over Smartlink ports, ip neighbor-flood must be enabled on respective SVI interfaces. - Smartlink uses ERPS copp class for flush packets. Limitations: - VSX, ISL and MCLAGs cannot be included in Smartlink groups. - Switches with both Smartlink and STP enabled exclude Smartlink ports from STP. - On switches with both Smartlink and STP enabled, loops involving Smartlink and STP are not detected. - On switches with both Smartlink and ERPS enabled, loops involving Smartlink and ERPS are not detected. - ERPS and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port. - Dynamic VLANs (MVRP) and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port. - Loop Protect and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port. - Multicast fast convergence is not supported. - Uplink Failure Detection (UFD) is not supported. - MIB and WebUI are not supported..

input_text: For 10000 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show smartlink group command do?
target_value: The show smartlink group command shows information for a specific Smartlink group.

target_value: VLAN interfaces: - VLAN interfaces are of type vlan. - You can use the DELETE method to delete a VLAN interface. Example of creating a VLAN interface: Method and URI: POST "/rest/v10.04/system/interfaces" Request body: { "name": "vlan2", "vlan_tag": "/rest/v10.04/system/vlans/2", "vrf": "/rest/v10.04/system/vrfs/default", "type": "vlan" } Write methods (POST, PUT) supported in read-only mode The following switch resources support write methods (POST, PUT, or both) even when the REST API access mode is set to read-only:

target_value: To troubleshoot, follow the documented procedure: 1. Ensure that you save the session cookie returned from the login request 2. Log out from one of the existing sessions 3. Repeat the command and send the correct session cookie or modify the script to send the correct. Commands shown in the procedure include: REST API | response | codes; https-server | | vrf mgmt.
